topic: Stop building AI tools. Start building AI processes.
hook_summary: Everyone is building AI products. The real money is in process, not product.

scenes.audio_text: Everyone's out here building AI tools. They're missing the point.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, medium close-up, indoors in a modern cafe with warm lighting, candid documentary style, natural daylight from window, locked-off camera, slight background blur.

scenes.audio_text: I spent six months building an AI scheduling bot. Got 12 users. Then I automated my client's onboarding process — 8 hours of manual work down to 20 minutes. They paid me $5,000.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, over-shoulder shot at a desk with a laptop and coffee, bookshelf in background, soft artificial light, slow push-in, documentary feel.

scenes.audio_text: The tool is a commodity. The process is the moat. Anyone can clone your chatbot. They can't clone your system.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, walking on a city street with blurred pedestrians, golden hour lighting, medium shot, slight handheld motion for realism.

scenes.audio_text: So stop building. Start systematizing. That's where the leverage lives.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, wide shot in a rooftop garden with skyline background, natural overcast light, locked-off camera, calm candid pose.
